ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "customer_name" TEXT /* Full name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  "complaint_channel" TEXT /* The channel through which the customer lodged the complaint */,
  "crm_id" INTEGER /* The unique identifier for the customer in the CRM system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "appliances" (
  "appliance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each appliance */,
  "appliance_type" TEXT /* Type of appliance (e.g., refrigerator, oven) */,
  "model_number" TEXT /* Model number of the appliance */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the appliance */,
  "appliance_category" TEXT /* A broader category for the appliance type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("appliance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "engineers" (
  "engineer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engineer */,
  "engineer_name" TEXT /* Full name of the engineer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the engineer */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the engineer */,
  "specialization" TEXT /* Specialization of the engineer (e.g., refrigerator, oven) */,
  "role" TEXT /* The role of the engineer */,
  "mobile_app_login" INTEGER /* A flag indicating whether the engineer has logged in to the mobile app */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* The timestamp of the engineer's last login to the mobile app */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engineer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "complaints" (
  "complain_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each complaint */,
  "complain_date" TEXT /* Date the complaint was lodged */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who lodged the complaint */,
  "appliance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the appliance that requires repair */,
  "engineer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the engineer assigned to the complaint */,
  "labour_hours" INTEGER /* Number of labor hours spent on the repair */,
  "travel_hours" INTEGER /* Number of travel hours spent on the repair */,
  "complaint_description" TEXT /* A brief description of the customer's complaint */,
  "complaint_status" TEXT /* The current status of the complaint */,
  "resolution" TEXT /* A description of the resolution provided to the customer */,
  "customer_satisfaction" INTEGER /* A rating of the customer's satisfaction with the repair */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("complain_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_complaints_engineer_id FOREIGN KEY ("engineer_id") REFERENCES engineers ("engineer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_complaints_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_complaints_appliance_id FOREIGN KEY ("appliance_id") REFERENCES appliances ("appliance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "assignments" (
  "assignment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each assignment */,
  "complain_id" INTEGER /* ID of the complaint being assigned */,
  "engineer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the engineer being assigned */,
  "assignment_date" TEXT /* Date the assignment was made */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the assignment */,
  "assignment_priority" TEXT /* The priority level of the assignment */,
  "estimated_completion_date" TEXT /* The estimated date of completion for the assignment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assignment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_assignments_engineer_id FOREIGN KEY ("engineer_id") REFERENCES engineers ("engineer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_assignments_complain_id FOREIGN KEY ("complain_id") REFERENCES complaints ("complain_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "complaint_channel" (
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each channel */,
  "channel_type" TEXT /* Type of channel (e.g., phone, email, online portal) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("channel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "repair_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "complain_id" INTEGER /* ID of the complaint */,
  "average_repair_time" REAL /* Average repair time for similar complaints */,
  "average_labor_hours" REAL /* Average labor hours spent on similar repairs */,
  "average_travel_hours" REAL /* Average travel hours spent on similar repairs */,
  "customer_satisfaction_rate" REAL /* Average customer satisfaction rate for similar repairs */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_repair_metrics_complain_id FOREIGN KEY ("complain_id") REFERENCES complaints ("complain_id")
)
